changes_made: Renamed k8s_admission_controller_data_sanitization_enforced to k8s_admission_data_sanitization_enforced for brevity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to secure data transfer. Network policies, ingress configurations, and service meshes are appropriate mechanisms for enforcing TLS and mTLS. Admission controllers can indeed enforce data sanitization policies. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a slight adjustment was made to the admission controller function for consistency and brevity.
naming_improvements: Shortened 'k8s_admission_controller_data_sanitization_enforced' to 'k8s_admission_data_sanitization_enforced' for consistency with other function names
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | data sanitization when transferring information b
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(25) focuses on ensuring secure data transfer, which in Kubernetes can be enforced through several mechanisms. NetworkPolicies can enforce TLS for secure communication between pods. Ingress controllers can be configured to require TLS for external communications. Service meshes like Istio or Linkerd can enforce mutual TLS (mTLS) for service-to-service communication. Additionally, admission controllers can be used to enforce data sanitization policies before data is transferred.
